title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer · History Defense
summary: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a history-rewrite plan and verification graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.
Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Rebase Replay · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

Which action demonstrates Rebase Replay without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.

content.text: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Rebase Replay result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: gitworkflows Manual — Controls integration, topic branch, merge, release, and distributed maintainer workflow guidance.

hints: Name the Rebase Replay precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Rebase moves existing commit objects without changing their IDs.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Rebase Replay evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Rebase Replay assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Rebase Replay has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Rewrite Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
